mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods where industrial production rises, or the signal loses predictive power during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 235.0, elevated inflation expectations trigger equity risk premium repricing and anticipated monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses explanatory power when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters where GDPC1 expands, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals contracting supply-side activity and weakening economic momentum, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of periods where industrial production drops by more than 0.5%, or the signal fails to achieve a t-statistic greater than 1.5 in walk-forward tests.
